root_text.an7.83:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā upāli yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā upāli bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an7.83:2.1: “Sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā saṅkhittena dhammaṁ desetu, yamahaṁ bhagavato dhammaṁ sutvā eko vūpakaṭṭho appamatto ātāpī pahitatto vihareyyan”ti.
root_text.an7.83:2.3: ‘ime dhammā na ekantanibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattantī’ti;
root_text.an7.83:2.6: Ye ca kho tvaṁ, upāli, dhamme jāneyyāsi:
root_text.an7.83:2.7: ‘ime dhammā ekantanibbidāya virāgāya nirodhāya upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattantī’ti;

translation_text.an7.83:1.1: Then Venerable Upāli went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an7.83:2.1: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.an7.83:2.3: certain things don’t lead solely to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.an7.83:2.4: Categorically, you should remember these things as
translation_text.an7.83:2.5: not the teaching, not the training, and not the Teacher’s instructions.
translation_text.an7.83:2.7: certain things do lead solely to disillusionment, dispassion, cessation, peace, insight, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.an7.83:2.8: Categorically, you should remember these things as
translation_text.an7.83:2.9: the teaching, the training, and the Teacher’s instructions.”
